content: Just as English language learners build long-term writing proficiency by structured reflection on their own draft outputs, LLM autonomous agents can improve their self-reflection mechanisms by adopting the graded, explicit reflection scaffolding used in high school literacy curricula, rather than relying solely on ad-hoc self-contradiction based reflection. This creates a dual-layer reflection framework where the agent first reflects on its output like a student, then uses inconsistent perspective contrast like current LLM reflection methods to refine that reflection.
